title: Lightweight Performance Cleanup

priorities.goal: Load only page-specific scripts on public pages.
priorities.risk: Unnecessary scripts can slow pages and create selector errors.
priorities.action: Use defer and avoid loading QA scripts by default.

priorities.goal: Keep the design system consistent without uncontrolled CSS growth.
priorities.risk: Large overlapping CSS files can make future visual changes fragile.
priorities.action: Document components and avoid duplicating card, hero, grid, and badge styles.

priorities.goal: Use appropriately sized assets for web display.
priorities.risk: Large images are the most likely source of slow loading.
priorities.action: Prefer SVG/WebP, dimensions, lazy loading, and optimized display files.

priorities.goal: Prevent duplicate or conflicting SEO/social tags.
priorities.risk: Duplicate metadata can weaken SEO and social previews.
priorities.action: Apply head metadata gradually and verify each priority page.

priorities.risk: Unneeded tools can add maintenance burden.
priorities.action: Avoid build tools, server code, and third-party scripts unless necessary.

recommendedNextPhase: F-6 — Site QA and Deployment Verification
